label: Marginal external costs of road transport
description: External costs imposed on society by an additional vehicle-km of travel. Used to value changes in traffic levels. Values vary by vehicle type, road type, and congestion level. Simplified averages shown here; TAG Data Book A5.4 provides full disaggregation.

congestion.note: Highly variable. Congestion costs dominate in urban peak conditions.

infrastructure_damage.note: Minimal for cars. HGVs cause 10,000x more damage per axle-pass.

accident_externality.note: External component only (costs to third parties not borne by the driver)

noise.note: Per vehicle-km. Depends on speed, road surface, building proximity.

air_quality.note: NOx and PM2.5 damage costs. Higher in urban areas with population exposure.

greenhouse_gas.note: Based on DESNZ non-traded carbon values applied to average CO2/km

congestion.note: HGVs cause proportionally more delay due to speed differentials

infrastructure_damage.note: Fourth-power law: road damage increases with the fourth power of axle weight

air_quality.note: Diesel particulates. Euro VI standards have significantly reduced this.

source.publication: TAG Data Book v2.02, Section A5.4

caveats: These are simplified averages. The TAG Data Book provides values disaggregated by 10+ vehicle classes, 5 road types, and 4 congestion bands.
Congestion costs are the largest and most variable component. Use location-specific data where available.
Air quality damage costs depend on population exposure (number of people within breathing distance of the road).
Phase 2 of the parameter database will add the full disaggregated matrix from TAG A5.4.
